zh.lifeSummary: William de la Heale於1100年出生在法國。他和Lady William de la Heale至少有1個兒子。他去世於1150年在英國戴文勒享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William de la Heale于1100年出生在法国。他和Lady William de la Heale至少有1个儿子。他去世于1150年在英国戴文勒享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Heale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Heal
meanings: topographic or habitational name for someone who lived in or by a nook or hollow, or at a place called with this word (from Middle English hele, Old English heale, dative singular of healh ‘nook or hollow’). Hele in Somerset derives from this word, but the surname arises chiefly from one or more of the many minor localities named Heal(e) or Hele in Devon. In southwestern England and Kent this is an equivalent of Hale .
in the southeast Midlands of England, perhaps a variant of   Hell   1.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: William de la Heale was born in 1100, in France. He had at least 1 son with Lady William de la Heale. He died in 1150, in Hele, Devon, England, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: William de la Heale est né en 1100, à France. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lady William de la Heale. Il est décédé en 1150, à Hele, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William de la Heale wurde 1100 in Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Lady William de la Heale mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1150 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Hele, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: William de la Heale è nato il nel 1100 nel Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lady William de la Heale. Morì nel 1150, nel Hele, Devon, England, all'età di 50 anni.

イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.lifeSummary: William de la Healeは1100年にフランスで生まれた。彼はLady William de la Healeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1150年にイギリスデボンヘルで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de la Heale(은)는 1100년 프랑스에서 태어났다. 그는 Lady William de la Heale(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1150년 영국 데폰 헬에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William de la Heale nasceu em 1100, em França. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Lady William de la Heale. Ele faleceu em 1150, em Hele, Devon, Inglaterra, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William de la Heale родился в 1100 г. в местности Франция. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Lady William de la Heale. Он умер в 1150 г. в местности хель, девон, Англия в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William de la Heale nació en 1100, en Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Lady William de la Heale. Murió en 1150, en Hele, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 50 años.
